value.title: Reddit looks to AI search as its next big opportunity
value.content: During the company's fourth-quarter earnings call on Thursday, it offered an update on its plans to merge traditional and AI search together and hinted that although search is not yet monetized, "it's an enormous market and opportunity."

value.title: Meta tests a stand-alone app for its AI-generated ‘Vibes’ videos
value.content: Launched last September, Vibes lets you create and share short-form AI-generated videos and access a dedicated feed that displays AI videos from others.

value.title: Substack confirms data breach affects users’ email addresses and phone numbers
value.content: Substack said that customer data was accessed in October 2025 but wasn't discovered until early February.

value.title: Spotify ventures into physical book sales, adds new audiobook features
value.content: Spotify users in the U.S. and the UK will soon be able to buy physical books in the app. There's also a new tool that syncs physical books to audiobooks.

value.title: Google’s Gemini app has surpassed 750M monthly active users
value.content: Google revealed a significant milestone for its Gemini app, announcing over 750 million monthly active users as it competes with ChatGPT and Meta AI.

value.title: Meet Gizmo: A TikTok for interactive, vibe-coded mini apps
value.content: Gizmo's app is like a TikTok for vibe-coded mini apps... and it's a lot of fun.

value.title: Tinder looks to AI to help fight ‘swipe fatigue’ and dating app burnout
value.content: Tinder is testing AI recommendations and insight from your Camera Roll for better matches.

value.title: After backlash, Adobe cancels Adobe Animate shutdown and puts app on ‘maintenance mode’
value.content: Adobe's announcement about discontinuing Animate was met with incredulity, disappointment, and anger. The company has changed its tune.

value.title: Spotify upgrades its lyrics feature with offline access, more translations
value.content: Spotify is revamping its lyrics feature, adding offline access for Premium users, global lyric translations, and a more prominent placement in the Now Playing view.

value.title: Xcode moves into agentic coding with deeper OpenAI and Anthropic integrations
value.content: Xcode 26.3 offers agentic coding capabilities with Anthropic's Claude Agent and OpenAI's Codex.

value.title: TikTok recovers from dip in usage that benefited rival apps following US ownership change
value.content: TikTok usage dropped following ownership change, benefiting rival apps. But now, its numbers are climbing again.

value.title: Fitbit founders launch AI platform to help families monitor their health
value.content: Luffu uses AI in the background to gather and organize family information, learn day-to-day patterns, and flag notable changes so families can stay aligned and address potential well-being issues.

value.title: India’s Supreme Court to WhatsApp: ‘You cannot play with the right to privacy’
value.content: India's top court is investigating WhatsApp’s data-sharing model, monopoly power, and user consent.

value.title: Firefox will soon let you block all of its generative AI features
value.content: Starting with Firefox 148 arriving later this month, users will find a new AI controls section within the desktop browser settings.

value.title: Grubhub waives delivery and service fees on restaurant orders over $50
value.content: In a bold move to close the gap with rivals, Grubhub is waiving delivery and service fees on restaurant orders over $50.

value.title: TikTok says its services are restored after the outage
value.content: TikTok says it finally solved all issues related to outages caused by last week's U.S. snowstorm.

value.title: Instagram might soon let you remove yourself from someone’s Close Friends list
value.content: Since the feature's launch in 2018, users haven't been able to remove themselves from someone else's Close Friends list.

value.title: Bluesky issues its first transparency report, noting rise in user reports and legal demands
value.content: Bluesky's first transparency reports tackle moderation, regulatory compliance, account takedowns, and more. The number of government legal requests went up fivefold.

value.title: Last 24 hours to grab your plus-one pass at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
value.content: Today is the last day to lock in a plus-one pass at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, happening on October 13-15 in San Francisco.
